# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Process Workflow (CI/CD)
on: [push, pull_request] # define events that trigger this job 
jobs:
    custom-build:   # name of our Job      
        runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # set the OS version to be latest for maximum efficiency and compatibility with most software used in a CI/CD setup. This could also depend on your specific needs, if you want it always run from Windows or macOS then use windows-latest; else linux best practice would still remain same
        steps:                     # define what commands we will do within the Job – these are made up of tasks that GitHub Actions can execute and return values.  
            - name: Checkout       # this is where actions/checkout@v2 (or equivalent) Action gets your repository checked out on runners, so you're not doing a full git clone here in the runner every time it runs for whatever reason! It only clones what changes have been made.
              uses: 'actions/checkout@master'  # using version of GitHub Actions actions and check-out to use that action with your repo so we can easily set things up across different runners        
            - name: Run Custom Commands   # This is where the custom command from user gets executed. Replace 'your_command1', ':param' etc inside this step block as per requirement       
              id: execute-custom-commands    # a unique ID for referencing it in other stages or outputs – not necessary here, but very useful when setting up branches/workflows        
                                                                           ,   with if else control and many more             . If you want to run shell command based on input parameters then use the steps block as follow:    - name : Run Custom Command     runs-on:. macos-.  Use actions.macos.latest or uses (based off latest macOS release) instead of ubuntu, which gives maximum compatibility with MacOS
              env:   # Define environment variables here if required by custom commands used in the step block      Variable must be set as "${{environments}}" when setting this inside GitHub Actions Workflows or else it will fail to execute.         Replace 'Variable1', ':Value' etc depending on requirement
                VARIABLE_NAME: $VARIALBE_VALUE    # Environment variables that are being used in the custom commands      Please replace VarialbeName and Value with your actual environment variable name & value .  Example, "MYSQL_PASSWORD":$(Build.artifactsFolder)/passphrase    
              run: |   # Here you put all shell command to be executed for this block (Replacing 'command1', ':param' etc inside here as per requirement)        Replace ${{steps.execute-custom-commands.outputs.'your_variable'} with the actual variable name used in step - execute custom commands
                  $COMMAND  # The shell command to run, replace "$VARIABLE" by your environment variables and `:` or `&amp;#x2018;&amp;#x2019;/your_command.sh) with the actual build parameters you have in place on user side
                  ${{environments}}  # The environmental variable that can be used to pass command into another step/job for instance (Example - `steps: docker-build, publish`). Replace "$VARIABLE" by your environment variables and use it inside here.          Please replace VarialbeName with env name as per requirement
                  $MY_OTHER_COMMAND # The other command to run using a separate variable   If needed you can put multiple commands then separated – each one on its own line      Replace 'Variable2' by your actual environment variables and use it here.       Example - ${{steps['docker-build'].outputs.'MY_VARIABLE'}
        outputs:    # This allows us to capture the output of our command, so that we can display them in this job or next steps – Replace 'OutputName' with your actual variable name and `:'Value` as per requirement.   For instance - ${{steps['execute-custom-commands'].outputs.'OUTPUTNAME'}
          value:    ${VARIABLE_NAME} # This is the output you capture from this block     Replace 'OutputName' by your actual variable name and `:'Value` as per requirement.   Example, '- ${{steps['execute-custom-commands'].outputs.'OUTPUTNAME'}
